remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Essentially zero for established T1DM (honeymoon phase occurs in 50-60% after diagnosis but lasts 3-12 months then ends); the disease is permanent autoimmune destruction of beta cells
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Closed-loop insulin delivery (artificial pancreas systems — Control-IQ, Omnipod 5) achieves 70-80% time-in-range vs 60% with standard therapy; teplizumab (anti-CD3 mAb) delays T1DM onset by 2+ years in at-risk individuals; islet cell transplantation achieves insulin independence in ~44% at 5 years (CITR data)
remission.gap_size: Moderate-large — closed-loop approaches are increasingly standard but true insulin independence is rare with any available therapy
remission.barrier_detail: Beta cell regeneration and transplantation face immune rejection barriers (all islet recipients require chronic immunosuppression); closed-loop systems dramatically improve quality of life and glycemic control but require intensive engagement and are expensive; teplizumab (FDA approved 2022) for delay is not a cure
remission.last_soc_change: 2022 — teplizumab (Tzield) FDA approved for Stage 2 T1DM (first immunotherapy to delay progression); 2023 closed-loop systems widely recommended; 2021 Omnipod 5 cleared; ADA continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) recommended for all T1DM 2020; insulin unchanged as backbone since 1921
remission.outdated_treatments: Multiple daily injection (MDI) with finger-stick monitoring in patients who would benefit from CGM + closed-loop (evidence clearly superior but access barriers remain); fixed insulin regimens without carbohydrate counting; NPH insulin use in settings where insulin analogues are available
remission.adjunctive_emerging: SGLT2 inhibitors as adjunct in T1DM (off-label, DKA risk with protocol; FDA rejected for T1D but widely used with mitigation strategies); GLP-1 agonists as adjunct (weight and dose reduction); ketogenic diet (very low-carb significantly reduces insulin requirements and variability)
remission.frontier_signal: Vertex VX-880 (stem-cell derived islet cells — Phase 1/2, insulin independence achieved in treated patients 2023); teplizumab for prevention in at-risk individuals; anti-thymocyte globulin (ATG) + GCSF for beta cell preservation; encapsulated islet transplantation (no immunosuppression needed — Phase 1 underway)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
